concepts.title: Determinant of a 3x3 matrix
concepts.description: The determinant of a 3x3 matrix can be found using the formula: ad - bc for matrices of the form [[a, b, c], [d, e, f], [g, h, i]].

concepts.title: Determinant of a matrix is zero if it is singular
concepts.description: If the determinant of a matrix is zero, it means that the matrix is singular and does not have an inverse.

concepts.title: Properties of Determinants
concepts.description: Determinants have the following properties: Determinant of a matrix is unchanged if the rows or columns are interchanged. Determinant of a matrix is multiplied by k if a multiple of a row (or column) is added to another row (or column). Determinant of a matrix is multiplied by k if a row (or column) is multiplied by k.

concepts.title: Mnemonic for the Determinant of a 3x3 matrix
concepts.description: Mnemonic: Across Down (ad - bc) or ADock (ad - bc)

concepts.title: Determinant of a matrix is not affected by the order of operations
concepts.description: Students often forget that the order of operations does not affect the determinant of a matrix. For example, determinant(a + b) is not equal to determinant(a) + determinant(b).

concepts.title: Determinant of a Skew-Symmetric Matrix is zero
concepts.description: The determinant of a skew-symmetric matrix (a matrix whose transpose is equal to its negative) is always zero.

concepts.title: Determinant of an upper triangular matrix
concepts.description: The determinant of an upper triangular matrix is equal to the product of the diagonal elements.

concepts.title: Determinant of a matrix is not affected by elementary row operations
concepts.description: Determinant of a matrix remains unchanged after applying elementary row operations such as interchanging two rows, multiplying a row by a non-zero constant, or adding a multiple of one row to another row.
